signals.idea: Cleanup and quality pass on timeline agent + gallery selection system. Three objectives: (1) ENGINEERING QUALITY: Review and refactor all code touched across the three recent features (timeline media selection context, agent task creation, gallery selection + multi-drag). Ensure excellent modular decomposition, clear responsibility boundaries, right levels of abstraction, no confusion about what happens where. This spans: useSelectedMediaClips, GallerySelectionContext, useLassoSelection, useModifierKeys, AgentChat attachment merging, useAgentSession send pipeline, create-task tool, clips.ts, generation.ts, tool-calls.ts, loop.ts, prompts.ts, useExternalDrop multi-drag, dragDrop.ts multi-drag type, MediaGalleryItem selection/drag changes, GenerationsPaneGallery selection wiring. (2) BUG FIX: Gallery selection highlighting (ring-2 ring-sky-400) is broken — it appears inconsistently, sometimes shows for a second then disappears, disappears on hover even when item is clicked/selected. This points to some state management or CSS issue in how isSelected prop flows through MediaGalleryItem or how the selection state interacts with hover/interaction states. Investigate and fix. (3) FEATURE: Make the AI timeline agent panel show over the locked pane state as well (currently it only shows when pane is unlocked).

unresolved_flags.concern: agent-tool-utils: Deduplication scope misses the actual shared helpers in `tools/create-task.ts` and assumes client/server sharing is impossible even though the repo already shares cross-runtime modules.

verification: Revised edge-function verification is still not executable as written because `npx tsc --noEmit` cannot type-check this Deno-based `ai-timeline-agent` code, and the configured Vitest runners still do not cover `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/**`.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: I ran `npx tsc --noEmit supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts` against the current repo, and it fails immediately on Deno URL imports, `.ts` extension imports, missing `Deno`, and unsupported module specifiers such as `https://deno.land/...` and `npm:groq-sdk`. That means the revised Step 9 command is technically the wrong verifier for `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/*` and would report environment/tooling errors rather than useful refactor regressions.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:24-37`. The revised Step 6 still groups `isRecord` with utilities 'currently duplicated across 4 files', but `create-task.ts` does not define or call `isRecord`; only `generation.ts` and `llm/messages.ts` do. The broader dedupe direction is fine, but that specific helper inventory is still inaccurate.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the cross-runtime sharing note against `supabase/functions/update-shot-pair-prompts/index.ts:6` and the `ai-timeline-agent` imports. The revised plan is more careful than before, but its statement that existing cross-runtime sharing in this repo is `import type` only is still factually too strong because there is already at least one runtime import from `src/` inside an edge function.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked `config/testing/vitest.config.ts:13-20`. The plan's final gate of `npx vitest run` will not cover any edge-function work because the default config includes only `src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}` and explicitly excludes `supabase/functions/**`, so the proposed verification does not actually touch `ai-timeline-agent` changes.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the two user-visible `must` goals for the selection ring and locked-pane chat. They describe the right product outcomes, but they are mostly manual UI behaviors rather than code-review-verifiable gates, so they are not fully testable from the listed automated checks alone.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the revised Step 6 against `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:24-37` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/generation.ts:22-39`. The plan now includes the create-task helper duplication, but it replaces the missing edge verification path with `npx tsc --noEmit`, which is not actually workable for these Deno edge files, so the revision still does not fully satisfy the edge-function part of the brief with a usable validation step.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/selectedClips.test.ts` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.test.ts`. The revised plan says the edge refactor has 'no automated test path', but this subsystem already has tests on disk; the real gap is that the configured runners do not include them, not that the subsystem is completely untested.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the new validation list in the revised plan against the repo test inventory. The added 10-file frontend suite now covers the previously-missed touched-area tests, but the plan still does not name or provide any command path for the two existing `ai-timeline-agent` tests under `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/`, so the edge supporting infrastructure is still only partially covered.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the actual helper usage in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:39-83`. This file only calls `asTrimmedString`, `asPositiveNumber`, and `asStringArray`, so the revised Step 6 import/update list should not treat `create-task.ts` as an `isRecord` caller when enumerating consumer updates.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the revised success criterion 'Edge function TypeScript compilation succeeds (`npx tsc --noEmit` on `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/`)'. On this repo that command is not a viable gate for Deno-authored edge modules, so this `should` criterion is not currently verifiable in the way the plan states it.

resolved_flags.concern: agent-chat-rendering: Plan analysis still frames the locked-pane bug around a generic transformed ancestor and does not explicitly account for `pane.transformClass` sliding the whole editor pane off-screen.
resolved_flags.resolution:
Revised plan addressing all 18 flags from gate feedback:

**Correctness fixes:**
- CSS transition fix corrected: `transition-all` → `transition-[opacity,transform]` (not `transition-[shadow,...]`) to avoid animating `box-shadow` property entirely (FLAG correctness-1)
- Track creation helper renamed from `createVisualTrackAtTop` to `createTrackAtTop(dataRef, kind: 'audio' | 'visual')` with explicit `kind` parameter matching existing line 300 behavior (FLAG-004, correctness-3)
- AgentChat portal analysis now describes precise mechanism: `pane.transformClass` applies `translate-y-0` (open) / `-translate-y-full` (closed) from `useSlidingPane.ts:252-253`, and even `translate-y-0` creates a containing block per CSS spec (FLAG-001, issue_hints-1)
- useExternalDrop dispatcher preserves caller-specific semantics: `trackId: undefined` for new-track-creation, `insertAtTop` forwarding per path (FLAG callers-1)
- Cross-runtime sharing clarified: existing imports are `import type` only (type-erased), not runtime. Client-side copies kept with explicit justification (FLAG correctness-2)

**Completeness fixes:**
- Dedup scope expanded to include `create-task.ts` alongside `generation.ts` and `messages.ts` — all 4 files with duplicated utils (FLAG-002, issue_hints-2, scope-2)
- Test validation list expanded from 5 to 10 files, including `AgentChat.test.tsx`, `useAgentSession.test.tsx`, `useExternalDrop.test.tsx`, `MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, `GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx` (FLAG-003, all_locations-1, scope-1)
- Edge function verification strategy: TypeScript compilation check (`npx tsc --noEmit`) since vitest excludes `supabase/functions/**` (FLAG all_locations-2)
- `useAgentSession.test.tsx` explicitly in validation list to guard send-pipeline caller contract (FLAG callers-2)
- Success criteria broadened: "all existing tests pass" via `npx vitest run`, not just named 5 (FLAG criteria_quality-1)

**Scope adjustments:**
- Dropped AgentChat.tsx decomposition (was Step 10): component is 499 lines, repo threshold is >600, and "line count alone is not the signal" per `refactoring_patterns.md:36` (FLAG-005)
- Selection ring and locked-pane criteria kept as `must` for product correctness but acknowledged as partially manual-verified (FLAG criteria_quality-2)

resolved_flags.concern: drop-handling: The proposed `createVisualTrackAtTop` extraction does not match all current callers because file drops create both audio and visual tracks depending on file type.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:146-155`, `src/shared/hooks/useSlidingPane.ts:244-278`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:420,447`. resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:379-454`. `handleAssetDrop` is called with materially different argument shapes across multi-generation, single-generation, file-upload, and asset-key paths, including `trackId` intentionally being `undefined` to force new-track creation and `insertAtTop` only being forwarded for certain cases, so the dispatcher refactor has to preserve those caller-specific semantics exactly.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the send pipeline caller contract in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:241-259` and its existing assertion in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx:43-84`. Refactoring `useAgentSession.ts` without explicitly keeping that caller contract in the verification plan risks silently changing how `generation_id` is serialized for selected attachments.
resolved_flags.resolution:
Revised plan addressing all 18 flags from gate feedback:

**Correctness fixes:**
- CSS transition fix corrected: `transition-all` → `transition-[opacity,transform]` (not `transition-[shadow,...]`) to avoid animating `box-shadow` property entirely (FLAG correctness-1)
- Track creation helper renamed from `createVisualTrackAtTop` to `createTrackAtTop(dataRef, kind: 'audio' | 'visual')` with explicit `kind` parameter matching existing line 300 behavior (FLAG-004, correctness-3)
- AgentChat portal analysis now describes precise mechanism: `pane.transformClass` applies `translate-y-0` (open) / `-translate-y-full` (closed) from `useSlidingPane.ts:252-253`, and even `translate-y-0` creates a containing block per CSS spec (FLAG-001, issue_hints-1)
- useExternalDrop dispatcher preserves caller-specific semantics: `trackId: undefined` for new-track-creation, `insertAtTop` forwarding per path (FLAG callers-1)
- Cross-runtime sharing clarified: existing imports are `import type` only (type-erased), not runtime. Client-side copies kept with explicit justification (FLAG correctness-2)

**Completeness fixes:**
- Dedup scope expanded to include `create-task.ts` alongside `generation.ts` and `messages.ts` — all 4 files with duplicated utils (FLAG-002, issue_hints-2, scope-2)
- Test validation list expanded from 5 to 10 files, including `AgentChat.test.tsx`, `useAgentSession.test.tsx`, `useExternalDrop.test.tsx`, `MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, `GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx` (FLAG-003, all_locations-1, scope-1)
- Edge function verification strategy: TypeScript compilation check (`npx tsc --noEmit`) since vitest excludes `supabase/functions/**` (FLAG all_locations-2)
- `useAgentSession.test.tsx` explicitly in validation list to guard send-pipeline caller contract (FLAG callers-2)
- Success criteria broadened: "all existing tests pass" via `npx vitest run`, not just named 5 (FLAG criteria_quality-1)

**Scope adjustments:**
- Dropped AgentChat.tsx decomposition (was Step 10): component is 499 lines, repo threshold is >600, and "line count alone is not the signal" per `refactoring_patterns.md:36` (FLAG-005)
- Selection ring and locked-pane criteria kept as `must` for product correctness but acknowledged as partially manual-verified (FLAG criteria_quality-2)

resolved_flags.concern: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?: Checked the repo's refactoring guide in `docs/structure_detail/refactoring_patterns.md`. The guide says components under roughly 600 lines should not be split on size alone and that line count is not the signal, while `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` is 499 lines, so Step 10 needs a stronger responsibility-based justification than the current size-driven framing.
resolved_flags.resolution: Checked the repo's refactoring guide in `docs/structure_detail/refactoring_patterns.md`. The guide says components under roughly 600 lines should not be split on size alone and that line count is not the signal, while `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` is 499 lines, so Step 10 needs a stronger responsibility-based justification than the current size-driven framing.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the repository's current test inventory. The `must` criterion saying 'All 5 existing test files pass' is under-scoped because there are more existing relevant tests than the five named in the plan, so it is not a complete yes/no gate for this work.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 27.5 -> 18.25. Plan deltas: 67.4%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 12. Open significant flags: 12.

items.criterion: Selection ring (ring-2 ring-sky-400) appears instantly on selected gallery items — CSS fix replaces transition-all with transition-[opacity,transform] at MediaGalleryItem.tsx:352

All 10 existing test files for touched areas pass: MediaGalleryItem.behavior, GenerationsPaneGallery, dragDrop, useLassoSelection, useModifierKeys, MediaGalleryGrid, GallerySelectionContext, AgentChat, useAgentSession, useExternalDrop
Full npx vitest run passes with no regressions
AgentChat renders via createPortal to document.body, making it visible at bottom-right viewport position in locked pane, unlocked pane, and full editor page
Multi-drag from gallery to timeline continues working (drag preview, drop handling, track creation)
Edge function changes are backward-compatible — no breaking changes to session schema or tool call format
No duplicated isRecord/asTrimmedString/asPositiveNumber/asStringArray across server-side edge function files (generation.ts, create-task.ts, messages.ts all import from utils.ts)
useExternalDrop.ts onTimelineDrop handler reduced from ~297 lines to a dispatcher under 50 lines with per-type handler functions
createTrackAtTop helper accepts kind parameter (audio/visual) and replaces 3 duplicated track creation blocks
loop.ts runAgentLoop function reduced to orchestration-only with extracted cleanAssistantText, executeToolCall, buildToolErrorTurn helpers
command-parser.ts parseCommand uses dispatch map instead of 15 if-branches
Edge function TypeScript compilation succeeds (npx tsc --noEmit on supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/)
AgentChat works correctly in full editor page, unlocked pane, and locked pane (manual smoke test)
Lasso selection, modifier-click toggle, and single-click selection all work in gallery (manual smoke test)
useAgentSession.test.tsx send-pipeline contract (generation_id serialization) passes unchanged

unresolved_flags.concern: agent-tool-utils: Deduplication scope misses the actual shared helpers in `tools/create-task.ts` and assumes client/server sharing is impossible even though the repo already shares cross-runtime modules.

unresolved_flags.concern: verification: Revised edge-function verification is still not executable as written because `npx tsc --noEmit` cannot type-check this Deno-based `ai-timeline-agent` code, and the configured Vitest runners still do not cover `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/**`.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts:1` imports `https://deno.land/std@0.224.0/http/server.ts`; `tsconfig.app.json` includes only `src`; an actual run of `npx tsc --noEmit supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts` in this repo fails on URL imports, `.ts` extension imports, and missing `Deno`; `config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts` still omits `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/**/*.test.ts`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: I ran `npx tsc --noEmit supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts` against the current repo, and it fails immediately on Deno URL imports, `.ts` extension imports, missing `Deno`, and unsupported module specifiers such as `https://deno.land/...` and `npm:groq-sdk`. That means the revised Step 9 command is technically the wrong verifier for `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/*` and would report environment/tooling errors rather than useful refactor regressions.
unresolved_flags.evidence: I ran `npx tsc --noEmit supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts` against the current repo, and it fails immediately on Deno URL imports, `.ts` extension imports, missing `Deno`, and unsupported module specifiers such as `https://deno.land/...` and `npm:groq-sdk`. That means the revised Step 9 command is technically the wrong verifier for `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/*` and would report environment/tooling errors rather than useful refactor regressions.

The revised Step 6 still groups `isRecord` with utilities 'currently duplicated across 4 files', but `create-task.ts` does not define or call `isRecord`; only `generation.ts` and `llm/messages.ts` do. The broader dedupe direction is fine, but that specific helper inventory is still inaccurate.
